heading: Reports filed with the Commission
label: 40104 Reports filed with the Commission
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)4010446 App.:1714., , ; , , ; , , .Pub. L. 98–237, § 1598 Stat. 84Pub. L. 98–595, § 3(b)(3)98 Stat. 3133Pub. L. 105–258, title I, § 113112 Stat. 1912Mar. 20, 1984Oct. 30, 1984Oct. 14, 1998

Amendments2018—Subsec. (a). amended subsec. (a) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “The Federal Maritime Commission may require a common carrier or an officer, receiver, trustee, lessee, agent, or employee of the carrier to file with the Commission a periodical or special report, an account, record, rate, or charge, or a memorandum of facts and transactions related to the business of the carrier. The report, account, record, rate, charge, or memorandum shall be made under oath if the Commission requires, and shall be filed in the form and within the time prescribed by the Commission.”Pub. L. 115–282

Amendments2018—Subsec. (a). amended subsec. (a) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “The Federal Maritime Commission may require a common carrier or an officer, receiver, trustee, lessee, agent, or employee of the carrier to file with the Commission a periodical or special report, an account, record, rate, or charge, or a memorandum of facts and transactions related to the business of the carrier. The report, account, record, rate, charge, or memorandum shall be made under oath if the Commission requires, and shall be filed in the form and within the time prescribed by the Commission.”Pub. L. 115–282

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Federal Maritime Commission may require a common carrier or marine terminal operator, or an officer, receiver, trustee, lessee, agent, or employee of the common carrier or marine terminal operator to file with the Commission a periodical or special report, an account, record, rate, or charge, or a memorandum of facts and transactions related to the business of the common carrier or marine terminal operator, as applicable.

children.label: A
children.text: be made under oath if the Commission requires; and

children.label: B
children.text: be filed in the form and within the time prescribed by the Commission.

children.label: A
children.text: limit the scope of any filing ordered under this section to fulfill the objective of the order; and

children.label: B
children.text: provide a reasonable period of time for respondents to respond based upon their capabilities and the scope of the order.

children.heading: .—Conference Minutes
children.label: b .—Conference Minutes
children.text: Conference minutes required to be filed with the Commission under this section may not be released to third parties or published by the Commission.
